title: Watched file stranded in inbox dir on UNIQUE constraint error
description:
In watch.rs, when process_file() calls post::create() (which uses plain INSERT, not INSERT OR IGNORE), a duplicate filesystem event for the same file causes a UNIQUE constraint violation (posts.source_ref is unique). The error propagates to watch_loop's Err branch, which logs it and leaves the file in the watch directory. Since notify only fires EventKind::Create once per file creation, the file is never retried and never moved to the published/ directory. The file is stuck in the inbox indefinitely with an opaque error log entry that gives no indication it was a dedup.

The post itself was already created by the first event, so data integrity is fine — but the operator sees a persistent error log and a stranded file with no clear explanation.

Fix: in watch.rs process_file() or watch_loop(), detect the UNIQUE constraint error (or check if a post with this source_ref already exists) and treat it as success — move the file to published/ rather than leaving it stranded.
close_reason: All pass 2 fixes applied: circuit breaker reset, persona-scoped 410, SSRF private IP block, tokio::fs, URL scheme validation, Content-Type, page clamp, watch dedup
